marketFocus: Risk-on recovery underway as US-Iran talks continue despite the failed Islamabad round. A senior US official confirmed 'talks are continuing even now and there is progress.' Sources say the parties were '80% there' before running into nuclear sticking points. A second round of face-to-face talks is being discussed for Thursday in Islamabad. Equities rebounded sharply: SPX +1.03%, NDX +1.07%, RUT +1.43%. DXY reversed initial strength to end with losses — 'Dollar reverses initial strength to end with losses as Antipodes outperform' (US FX Wrap).

Oil pulled back from the overnight spike: WTI settled at $99.08/bbl (+$2.51), Brent at $99.36/bbl (+$4.16) — well off the $103+ highs. The retreat coincided with optimism over a potential deal. EUR/USD retained a firm footing above 1.1700. GBP/USD reclaimed 1.3500. USD/JPY trickled toward 159.00 as dollar dwindled. Antipodeans outperformed — NZD pricing in 82bps of RBNZ hikes by year-end (up from 75bps last week). AUD flat vs USD but RBA Hauser struck a hawkish lean.

Today's key event: USD Core PPI m/m + PPI m/m at 14:30 FFT — CLOSE POS. BEFORE event. Speakers: BoE Bailey, Mann, Greene; ECB Lane, Cipollone, Lagarde; Fed Goolsbee, Barr, Collins, Barkin; BOC Macklem. Earnings: JPMorgan Chase, BlackRock, Citi, J&J, Wells Fargo. AUD Employment (Thu 03:30 FFT) — TONIGHT badge activates from 16:00 FFT Wednesday.

USD.tone: Dollar Reversed Initial Strength — Risk-On Unwind Drives DXY Lower; PPI Data Key Today
USD.summary: USD is Bearish today. The dollar reversed its initial risk-off strength as US-Iran diplomatic progress emerged — 'Dollar reverses initial strength to end with losses as Antipodes outperform' (US FX Wrap). DXY is little changed after weakening yesterday, with the market now pricing a deal as more likely than not. Fed's Miran (dovish dissenter) said he 'expects inflation to be close to target in a year' and sees no reason to think oil prices will remain elevated — a dovish signal that caps USD upside. Today's PPI data at 14:30 FFT is the key risk: a soft print would accelerate USD selling. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: Dollar reversed initial strength — risk-on unwind as US-Iran talks continue with progress
Fed Miran (dovish): 'expects inflation close to target in a year' — caps USD upside
DXY little changed after weakening — market pricing deal as more likely
USD Core PPI m/m + PPI m/m at 14:30 FFT — key risk event today (CLOSE POS. BEFORE)
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ALIGNED

EUR.tone: EUR/USD Firm Above 1.1700 — Energy Importer Benefits from Oil Pullback; ECB Speakers Today
EUR.summary: EUR is Bullish today. EUR/USD retained a firm footing above 1.1700 after benefiting from the recent retreat in the dollar and the pullback in oil from the overnight highs. The Eurozone's energy importer status, which was a headwind when oil surged, now becomes a tailwind as oil pulls back on deal optimism. Bund futures returned to the 125.00 level, clawing back recent lost ground. ECB's Lane, Cipollone and Lagarde speak today — any hawkish tone would reinforce EUR strength. EU Commission President von der Leyen announced proposals for energy price measures on April 22nd. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: EUR/USD firm above 1.1700 — benefits from dollar retreat and oil pullback
Bund futures returned to 125.00 — bond market stabilising as deal optimism grows
ECB Lane, Cipollone, Lagarde speak today — watch for hawkish tone
EU Commission energy price measures proposal on April 22nd — structural support
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED

GBP.tone: GBP/USD Reclaimed 1.3500 — Risk-On Recovery; BoE Bailey, Mann, Greene All Speaking Today
GBP.summary: GBP is Bullish today. GBP/USD held on to its spoils after reclaiming the 1.3500 status, with little reaction to BoE Governor Bailey's comments on financial stability. Sterling's risk-sensitive character is working in its favour as risk-on conditions return — the same dynamic that drove GBP higher in the ceasefire rally is back in play. UK BRC Retail Sales YoY (Mar) came in at 3.1% vs 0.9% expected — a strong beat that supports the BOE hawkish hold narrative. BoE Bailey, Mann and Greene all speak today. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: GBP/USD reclaimed 1.3500 — risk-on character supports Sterling in recovery
UK BRC Retail Sales YoY (Mar) 3.1% vs 0.9% exp — strong beat supports BOE hawkish hold
BoE Bailey, Mann, Greene all speaking today — watch for hawkish tone
Risk-sensitive character working in GBP's favour as risk-on conditions return
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED

JPY.tone: USD/JPY Trickled Toward 159 — Lower Oil Only Mild Support; JGB 20yr Auction Strong
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. USD/JPY trickled toward the 159.00 level as the dollar dwindled, but the yen's recovery is limited — lower oil provides only mild support given the BOJ's still-accommodative stance. The 20yr JGB auction showed the highest bid/cover ratio since 2019 (4.82x), reflecting strong domestic demand for JGBs, but this has not translated into meaningful yen strength. BOJ Governor Ueda said the economy is 'recovering moderately but with some weakness' and that underlying inflation is 'gradually accelerating toward the target' — no fresh hawkish signal. Japanese PM Takaichi cautioned Economy Minister Akazawa not to comment further on the BOJ. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: USD/JPY trickled toward 159 — lower oil only mild support; BOJ still accommodative
JGB 20yr auction b/c 4.82x (highest since 2019) — strong domestic demand, limited FX impact
BOJ Ueda: 'economy recovering moderately but with some weakness' — no fresh hawkish signal
Japanese PM Takaichi cautioned Akazawa not to comment on BOJ — verbal intervention risk capped
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = ALIGNED

AUD.tone: Antipodes Outperform — ASX 200 Lifted by Tech and Miners; RBA Hauser Hawkish Lean
AUD.summary: AUD is Bullish today. The Antipodeans outperformed in the risk-on recovery — 'Antipodes outperform' per the US FX Wrap. ASX 200 was lifted by outperformance in tech and miners, though the upside was capped by deterioration in Australian Consumer Sentiment and Business Confidence surveys. RBA Deputy Governor Hauser said he is 'not sure interest rates are at the right level to tame inflation' and that 'inflation in Australia is too high' — a hawkish lean that supports AUD. Mixed Chinese trade data (exports disappointed, imports surged) had a limited impact. AUD Employment + Unemployment Rate on Thursday at 03:30 FFT — TONIGHT badge activates from 16:00 FFT Wednesday. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: Antipodes outperform in risk-on recovery — ASX 200 lifted by tech and miners
RBA Hauser: 'not sure rates at right level to tame inflation' — hawkish lean supports AUD
Mixed Chinese trade data: exports disappointed, imports surged — limited net impact
Consumer Sentiment and Business Confidence surveys deteriorated — caps upside
AUD Employment + Unemployment Rate Thu 03:30 FFT — TONIGHT badge from 16:00 FFT Wed

CAD.tone: Oil Still Near $99/bbl — Petro-Proxy Support Intact; BOC Macklem Speaks Today
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. Oil remains elevated near $99/bbl (WTI settled $99.08) despite pulling back from the overnight $103+ highs — the petro-proxy support for CAD remains intact. Canadian PM Carney received a parliamentary majority following a win by liberals in the special election — a politically stable outcome that removes near-term domestic uncertainty. BOC Governor Macklem speaks today — watch for any shift in tone given the oil reversal and the ongoing geopolitical uncertainty. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bullish = ❌ NO alignment — intraday only.
drivers: Oil still near $99/bbl (WTI $99.08 settlement) — petro-proxy support intact for CAD
Canadian PM Carney received parliamentary majority — political stability removes uncertainty
BOC Governor Macklem speaks today — watch for tone given oil reversal
Oil pulled back from $103+ highs on deal optimism — upside risk if talks collapse
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment — intraday only

CHF.tone: Asymmetric Haven Lagging in Risk-On Recovery — SNB Intervention Cap Limits Upside
CHF.summary: CHF is Neutral today. The Franc is lagging in the risk-on recovery — as risk appetite improves, the haven bid for CHF fades. The SNB intervention cap limits excessive CHF strength, and the market has consistently preferred USD as the primary haven in this conflict cycle. Neither a strong positive nor negative driver dominates. SNB Chairman Schlegel speaks Wednesday — the key event for CHF this week. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no directional alignment.
drivers: Asymmetric haven lagging in risk-on recovery — haven bid fades as risk appetite improves
SNB intervention cap limits excessive CHF strength
USD preferred as haven over CHF — yield advantage and intervention risk
SNB Schlegel speaks Wed — key event for CHF this week
Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no directional alignment

NZD.tone: NZD G10 Outperformer — 82bps RBNZ Hikes Priced by Year-End; Risk-On Recovery Amplifies
NZD.summary: NZD is Bullish today. NZD was the G10 outperformer in the risk-on recovery, with 82bps of RBNZ rate hikes now priced by year-end — up from 75bps last week. The combination of the RBNZ hawkish surprise and the risk-on environment is a powerful tailwind. RBNZ's Breman speaks today — any reinforcement of the hawkish stance would extend NZD strength. Antipodeans were rangebound during the Asian session amid positive risk appetite, but the directional bias is clearly bullish. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: NZD G10 outperformer — 82bps RBNZ hikes priced by year-end (up from 75bps last week)
Risk-on recovery amplifies RBNZ hawkish story — dual tailwind
RBNZ Breman speaks today — hawkish reinforcement would extend NZD strength
Antipodeans rangebound in Asia but directional bias clearly bullish
Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED
